message:
fix(db,tui,tools): harden pool diagnostics, search consistency, and TUI rendering

Broad reliability and quality improvements across the DB layer,
search consistency module, TUI screens, and reservation tools.

Key changes in mcp-agent-mail-db:
- pool.rs: improve connection pool diagnostics and error reporting
- search_consistency.rs: add additional consistency invariant checks
  for search index synchronization
- Refactor test harness setup across 18 test files to use streamlined
  DB initialization, reducing boilerplate and improving maintainability
- query_integration: simplify test setup and query construction
- timeout_backpressure: improve timeout handling and test assertions
- identity_fts_cleanup: restructure FTS cleanup test flow

Key changes in mcp-agent-mail-search-core:
- consistency.rs: extend consistency verification with new invariant
  checks and improved error context

Key changes in mcp-agent-mail-server:
- console.rs: expand TUI console functionality with improved screen
  transitions, better error display, and enhanced user interaction
- lib.rs: add new server configuration options and initialization paths
- TUI screens (dashboard, projects, threads, analytics): improve
  rendering logic, fix layout edge cases, and add new display features

Key changes in mcp-agent-mail-tools:
- reservations.rs: add validation and edge case handling for
  file reservation operations

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"br-2ei.9.6","title":"E2E: HTTP server parity suite (auth/rate-limit/streamable/mail/CORS/logging)","description":"Add a single HTTP-focused E2E script that exercises the *user-visible* HTTP server surface end-to-end, using the shared harness (br-2ei.9.1).

Script:
- scripts/e2e_http.sh

Coverage (must be parity-oriented, not "best-effort"):
1. Health + well-known endpoints: /health/* and /.well-known/* payloads + status codes.
2. Bearer auth:
   - Authorization required when configured
   - OPTIONS + /health bypass
   - constant-time compare (behavioral checks)
3. JWT auth:
   - HS256 and JWKS modes (use deterministic test vectors)
   - aud/iss validation
   - role claim parsing
   - failure semantics always 401 {"detail":"Unauthorized"}
4. Rate limiting:
   - Redis backend allow/deny sequences
   - Redis failure -> memory fallback
   - identity derivation (sub vs host)
5. Peer addr + localhost bypass:
   - localhost allowed only when NO forwarded headers
   - IPv4/IPv6/IPv4-mapped IPv6 cases
6. Streamable HTTP MCP:
   - Accept/Content-Type normalization
   - base path /api and /api/ semantics
   - passthrough behavior
   - localhost Authorization injection when configured
7. /mail SSR UI:
   - fetch key pages and assert DOM markers
   - sanitize XSS payloads
8. CORS:
   - OPTIONS preflight headers
   - default allow-* semantics
9. Request logging + OTEL:
   - logs emitted only when enabled
   - OTEL misconfig doesn't crash
10. Tool filtering profiles (context reduction):
   - With filtering disabled: tool list matches full baseline.
   - With filtering enabled (minimal + one custom include/exclude case):
     - `tools/list` is filtered
     - `resource://tooling/directory` reflects filtered tools/clusters
11. Instrumentation (query tracking):
   - With instrumentation enabled, at least one DB-backed tool call emits a `tool_query_stats` log entry.
12. ACK TTL background worker (+ optional escalation):
   - With ACK_TTL enabled (ttl=0), create an ack_required message and assert logs contain `ack_overdue`.
   - With escalation mode file_reservation, assert a reservation is created (resource read or DB query) without crashing.

Artifacts/logging:
- tests/artifacts/http/<timestamp>/
- For each case: record request (curl args), response status, headers, body, and expected-vs-actual.
- When applicable, include server stdout/stderr logs and config env snapshot (redacting secrets).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. scripts/e2e_http.sh exists and can be run via scripts/e2e_test.sh http.
2. Script exits non-zero on first mismatch and prints expected vs actual.
3. Artifacts include per-case HTTP transcripts + server logs.
4. Script is deterministic (no random ports without recording, stable vectors).
5. Tool filtering checks cover at least: disabled baseline + minimal profile + one custom include/exclude case.
6. Instrumentation check asserts presence of `tool_query_stats` in captured server logs.
7.  {"id":"br-2ei.9.8","title":"E2E: Notifications signal files","description":"E2E: Notifications (signal files) parity.

Scope:
- Start server with notifications enabled.
- send_message to multiple recipients (to/cc/bcc) and assert signal files for to+cc only.
- fetch_inbox clears signal for that agent; verify pending list updates.
- debounce behavior: repeated sends within window do not spam signals (per legacy spec).

Logging/artifacts:
- Capture filesystem snapshots of signals dir before/after.
- Store artifacts under tests/artifacts/notifications/<timestamp>/.
- Emit expected vs actual file lists + JSON diffs on mismatch.

Dependencies:
- br-2ei.9.1 (E2E harness)
- br-2ei.12.2 (Notifications impl)
- br-2ei.12.3 (Notifications tests/spec)

## Acceptance Criteria
1. E2E test runs via `scripts/e2e_test.sh` notifications path and uses temp dirs (no destructive commands).
2. After send_message, signals are emitted for `to` + `cc` recipients only; `bcc` produces none.
3. fetch_inbox clears the signal for that agent and the pending list is updated accordingly.
4. Debounce behavior is verified: repeated sends within the debounce window do not create duplicate signal entries (assert via file list + timestamps or sequence numbers).
5.  {"id":"br-2f1mx","title":"T7.2: Implement HTML/SVG/Text screen export","description":"Add screen export capabilities using frankentui's export module. Operators can save the
current screen state to a file for sharing or documentation.

KEYBINDINGS:
- Ctrl+E: Export menu (choose format)
  - 'h': Export as HTML
  - 's': Export as SVG
  - 't': Export as plain text

EXPORT BEHAVIOR:
- Captures current frame buffer
- Applies theme colors in HTML/SVG output
- Saves to $AM_EXPORT_DIR (default: ~/.mcp_agent_mail/exports/)
- Filename: am_export_{screen}_{timestamp}.{ext}
- Toast: "Exported to {path}"

HTML EXPORT:
```html
<div style="font-family: monospace; background: #1a1a2e; color: #e0e0e0;">
  <!-- Full screen render with inline CSS for colors -->
</div>
```

 {"id":"br-la8ts","title":"Track 3: Context & Discovery — robot thread, search, message, navigate","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:16:27.237636045Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:20:06.361443649Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T06:20:06.361373408Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-la8ts","depends_on_id":"br-2ro9j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":905,"issue_id":"br-la8ts","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# Track 3: Context & Discovery Commands

## T3.1: `am robot thread <id>`

Render a complete conversation thread in agent-consumable format.
This is where Markdown format shines — conversations are natural in Markdown.

Default format: Markdown (override with --format toon|json)

Output:
```markdown
## Thread: FEAT-123 — Add authentication layer
**Messages**: 8 | **Participants**: BlueLake, RedFox, GreenCastle | **Last activity**: 5m ago

---

### [1] BlueLake → RedFox, GreenCastle | 2h ago | importance: high | ack: required
**Subject**: [FEAT-123] Starting auth implementation

I'm taking on the authentication feature. Planning to use JWT with JWKS.
Will need to modify: src/auth/**, src/middleware/**, tests/auth/**

---

### [2] RedFox → BlueLake | 1h45m ago | importance: normal | ack: done ✓
**Subject**: Re: [FEAT-123] Starting auth implementation

Sounds good. I have reservations on src/middleware/** — let me release those first.

---
(... remaining messages ...)
```

Flags:
  --limit N          Max messages (default: all)
  --include-bodies   Always include bodies (default: true for robot)
  --since <ts>       Only messages after timestamp
  --format md        Markdown (default for this command)

## T3.2: `am robot search <query>`

Full-text search with faceted results. More powerful than `am mail search`
because it includes relevance scores and aggregated facets.

```
am robot search "authentication JWT"
am robot search "deploy" --kind messages --importance urgent
am robot search "reservation conflict" --since 24h
```

Output:
```
_meta.query: authentication JWT
_meta.total_results: 12
_meta.elapsed_ms: 45

facets:
  by_thread: FEAT-123(5), AUTH-001(3), MISC(4)
  by_agent: BlueLake(4), RedFox(3), GreenCastle(5)
  by_importance: high(3), normal(8), urgent(1)

results[10]{id,relevance,from,subject,thread,snippet,age}:
  201,0.95,BlueLake,JWT implementation plan,FEAT-123,"...JWT with JWKS rotation...",2h
  198,0.87,RedFox,Auth middleware review,FEAT-123,"...middleware chain for auth...",3h
  ...
```

## T3.3: `am robot message <id>`

Single message with full context: body, thread position, adjacent messages.
Useful when an agent gets a message ID and wants to understand context.

Output includes:
- Full message body
- Thread ID and position (message 3 of 8)
- Previous message summary (for reply context)
- Next message summary (if exists)
- Sender info (program, model)
- Attachments list

## T3.4: `am robot navigate <uri>`

Resolve any MCP resource URI and return its data in robot format.
This is the "universal accessor" — any resource:// URI that the TUI
can deep-link to, the agent can access via this command.

```
am robot navigate resource://inbox/BlueLake
am robot navigate resource://thread/FEAT-123
am robot navigate resource://tooling/metrics
am robot navigate resource://views/ack-overdue/BlueLake
```

This is essentially a CLI wrapper around the MCP resource system,
but with robot-friendly formatting.

Candidate churn path is in /dp/asupersync/src/runtime/scheduler/three_lane.rs::ThreeLaneWorker::run_loop and drive_io_phase.

Key locations:
- drive_io_phase computes io_timeout=Duration::ZERO when fast_queue non-empty (lines ~1665-1678), then io.try_turn_with(io_timeout).
